features.description: Dashboard displays all active Claude Code sessions with real-time status indicators (active, waiting for input, waiting for permission, completed, errored). Sessions auto-appear when started and update without page refresh.
verification_steps: Step 1: Start 3 Claude Code sessions with different --name values
Step 2: Open the dashboard in a browser
Step 3: Verify all 3 sessions appear as cards/rows with correct names and 'active' status
Step 4: Trigger a permission prompt in one session — verify the dashboard updates to 'waiting for permission' within 2 seconds
Step 5: Complete a session — verify it shows 'completed' status
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12. WebSocket via Socket.io.

features.description: Desktop toast notifications via Windows notification system when any session changes to a state requiring user attention (permission request, input needed, error). Notification includes session name and what's needed.
verification_steps: Step 1: Start a Claude Code session with the Command Centre server running
Step 2: Trigger a permission prompt in the session
Step 3: Verify a Windows toast notification appears within 3 seconds
Step 4: Verify the notification shows the session name and the tool requesting permission
Step 5: Verify clicking the notification brings focus to the dashboard (or relevant terminal)
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12. Uses node-notifier, clickable.

features.description: Permission approval/denial from the dashboard. When a session is waiting for permission, the dashboard shows the tool name, input summary, and Approve/Deny buttons. Clicking Approve/Deny sends the response back to the Claude Code session.
verification_steps: Step 1: Start a Claude Code session that will trigger a permission prompt
Step 2: When the permission prompt appears, verify the dashboard shows tool name and input details
Step 3: Click 'Approve' on the dashboard
Step 4: Verify the Claude Code session receives the approval and continues execution
Step 5: Repeat with 'Deny' — verify the session receives the denial and handles it gracefully
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12. 30s timeout falls through to CLI.

features.description: Session naming and labelling. Each session card shows a human-readable name (from --name flag or project folder name), the project directory, how long it's been running, and a colour-coded status badge.
verification_steps: Step 1: Start a session with --name 'Auth Refactor'
Step 2: Start a session without --name (should fall back to project folder name)
Step 3: Verify both sessions show correct names on the dashboard
Step 4: Verify elapsed time updates in real-time
Step 5: Verify status badges are colour-coded (green=active, amber=waiting, red=error, grey=completed)
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12. Includes rename (F032) and start time (F033).

features.description: Event receiver server (Express + Socket.io) that accepts HTTP POST events from Claude Code hooks, tracks session state, and broadcasts updates to connected dashboard clients via WebSocket.
verification_steps: Step 1: Start the server with npm start
Step 2: Send a mock SessionStart event via curl to the hook endpoint
Step 3: Verify the server logs the event and creates a session record
Step 4: Send a mock PreToolUse event for the same session
Step 5: Verify the session state updates to 'waiting'
Step 6: Connect a WebSocket client and verify it receives real-time event broadcasts
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12. Running on localhost:4111.

features.description: Live activity feed showing recent events across all sessions in chronological order.
verification_steps: Step 1: Start 2 sessions generating events
Step 2: Open the dashboard activity feed
Step 3: Verify events from both sessions appear interleaved chronologically
Step 4: Verify each event shows session name, event type, and details
Step 5: Verify the feed auto-scrolls to show new events
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12.

features.description: Validate that Claude Code HTTP hooks can be used bidirectionally for approve/deny.
verification_steps: Step 1: Configure a PreToolUse HTTP hook pointing at a test server
Step 2: Trigger a tool use and log the payload
Step 3: Return approve/deny/ask responses and observe behaviour
Step 4: Document the payload format and response schema
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-11. Fully validated.

features.description: Setup script that injects HTTP hook configuration into Claude Code settings.
verification_steps: Step 1: Run setup command
Step 2: Verify hook entries added to ~/.claude/settings.json
Step 3: Verify existing settings preserved
Step 4: Start a session and verify events flow to server
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12. ISSUE: Launcher settings sync overwrites these hooks. Must be added to cloud template.

features.description: Portable configuration with sensible defaults. config.json auto-created on first run.
verification_steps: Step 1: Run server with no config.json
Step 2: Verify config auto-created with defaults
Step 3: Modify port, verify change takes effect on restart
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12.

features.description: Clickable toast notifications that open the dashboard in the browser.
verification_steps: Step 1: Trigger a toast notification
Step 2: Click it
Step 3: Verify dashboard opens in browser
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12.

features.description: Dismiss/remove completed sessions from the dashboard.
verification_steps: Step 1: Complete a session
Step 2: Click dismiss button on card
Step 3: Verify card removed from dashboard
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12.

features.description: Session transcript panel with full JSONL parsing and live polling.
verification_steps: Step 1: Click a session card
Step 2: Verify transcript panel opens showing conversation history
Step 3: Verify new messages appear in real-time
Step 4: Verify scroll-lock behaviour (auto-scroll pauses when reading history)
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12. Includes markdown rendering.

features.description: Text input to dashboard-managed sessions via CLI stream-json approach.
verification_steps: Step 1: Launch a dashboard-managed session
Step 2: Type a message in the input bar
Step 3: Verify Claude receives it and responds
Step 4: Verify streaming output appears in transcript
Step 5: Send a follow-up message (--resume)
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-13. Uses bash -c 'echo | claude -p' pattern. Known limitation: bypasses launcher.

features.description: Click-to-terminal focus — brings CLI terminal window to foreground from dashboard.
verification_steps: Step 1: Click 'Open in Terminal' link in transcript panel
Step 2: Verify the CLI terminal window comes to foreground
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12. Win32 SetForegroundWindow with Alt key trick.

features.description: Transcript scroll-lock — incremental DOM append preserves scroll position.
verification_steps: Step 1: Open transcript, scroll up to read history
Step 2: Verify new messages do not reset scroll position
Step 3: Scroll to bottom, verify auto-follow resumes
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12. F037 regression fix applied but needs live verification.

features.description: Rename/label sessions from dashboard via double-click inline edit.
verification_steps: Step 1: Double-click session name on card
Step 2: Type new name, press Enter
Step 3: Verify name updates on card and sidebar
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12.

features.description: Show session start time on cards and sidebar.
verification_steps: Step 1: Start a session
Step 2: Verify start time appears on session card
features.notes: Completed 2026-04-12.

features.description: Launcher auto-starts Command Centre server. On launcher startup, health-check localhost:4111/healthz. If down, spawn server as detached background process. If up, skip. Also: add CC HTTP hooks to the cloud settings template so they survive settings sync.
verification_steps: Step 1: Stop the CC server if running
Step 2: Run the launcher
Step 3: Verify the launcher reports 'Command Centre started' or 'Command Centre already running'
Step 4: Verify localhost:4111/healthz returns 200
Step 5: Run the launcher again — verify it skips (server already up)
Step 6: Start a Claude session — verify hooks fire and session appears on dashboard
Step 7: Verify HTTP hooks are present in the cloud template (claude-settings.json)
features.notes: CRITICAL FINDING: Launcher settings-sync.ts does a full overwrite of ~/.claude/settings.json from the cloud template. HTTP hooks must be added to the cloud template, not just injected locally. Two codebases affected: launcher (claude-workspace/launcher/) and Command Centre.

features.description: New Session invokes launcher instead of raw claude. Redesign the New Session modal to offer 'Open Launcher' as primary action (opens terminal running launcher with full project/MCP/harness support). Keep 'Quick Launch' as advanced collapsible for dashboard-managed sessions.
verification_steps: Step 1: Click 'New Session' on dashboard
Step 2: Click 'Open Launcher' — verify a terminal window opens running the launcher
Step 3: Complete the launcher flow (project, MCPs, harness) — verify session appears on dashboard
Step 4: Click 'Quick Launch' (advanced) — verify dashboard-managed session still works
Step 5: Verify the modal clearly explains the difference between the two options
features.notes: Merges old F005 and F015. Primary path = launcher in terminal. Advanced path = dashboard-managed (existing F029 flow).

features.description: Server process management. Keep the CC server running persistently — auto-restart on crash, survive terminal closure. Evaluate options: pm2, Windows scheduled task, shell profile startup script, or node-windows service wrapper.
verification_steps: Step 1: Start the server via the chosen mechanism
Step 2: Close the terminal that started it — verify server is still running (healthz responds)
Step 3: Kill the server process — verify it auto-restarts within 5 seconds
Step 4: Reboot the machine — verify server starts automatically on login
Step 5: Check server logs are being written to a file (not lost with terminal)
features.notes: Needs design decision on approach. pm2 is simplest but adds a dependency. node-windows creates a proper Windows service but is more complex.

features.description: Graceful handling of server not running. Verify Claude Code sessions work normally when CC server is down. Confirm hooks fail silently. Tune hook timeout to prevent session slowdown.
verification_steps: Step 1: Stop the CC server
Step 2: Start a Claude Code session
Step 3: Use several tools (Read, Bash, Edit) — verify no errors or slowdowns
Step 4: Time the hook timeout — verify it's under 2 seconds per tool call
Step 5: Start the CC server mid-session — verify the session registers on next hook event
Step 6: Check Claude Code's stderr/stdout for any hook failure warnings
features.notes: Mostly verification. May need to add a short timeout (e.g., 2s) to the HTTP hook config to prevent slowdown. Current setup has no explicit timeout on non-PreToolUse hooks.

features.description: Session kill from dashboard. Kill button on session cards with confirmation dialog. Handle both dashboard-managed sessions (child process kill) and terminal sessions (PID lookup via session tracking).
verification_steps: Step 1: Start a dashboard-managed session
Step 2: Click kill button — verify confirmation dialog appears
Step 3: Confirm kill — verify session stops and card updates to 'completed/errored'
Step 4: Start a terminal session
Step 5: Click kill button — verify it attempts to terminate (may not work for all terminal sessions)
Step 6: Verify the dashboard handles 'cannot kill' gracefully with a helpful message
features.notes: Dashboard-managed sessions are easy (ChildProcess.kill()). Terminal sessions are harder — need PID tracking or graceful 'go to terminal to stop' fallback.

features.description: Session pause/resume from dashboard. Pause a running session to stop token consumption, resume later. For dashboard-managed sessions, this means not sending the next prompt. For terminal sessions, may require Agent SDK or be infeasible.
verification_steps: Step 1: Start a dashboard-managed session that is actively working
Step 2: Click pause — verify the session stops making API calls
Step 3: Click resume — verify the session continues from where it left off
Step 4: For terminal sessions, verify a helpful message explains this isn't supported
Step 5: Verify paused state is visually distinct (new status badge colour)
features.notes: Feasibility TBC. Claude -p sessions are one-shot (prompt → response). 'Pause' may not be meaningful until Agent SDK is used. May need to descope to dashboard-managed only or defer entirely.

features.description: Cost and token tracking per session. Display token count and estimated USD cost on session cards. Aggregate summary in metrics bar. Source data from transcript JSONL files (usage fields) or hook payloads.
verification_steps: Step 1: Start a session and let it make several API calls
Step 2: Verify the session card shows token count and estimated cost
Step 3: Start a second session
Step 4: Verify the metrics bar shows combined totals
Step 5: Verify values update as sessions progress
Step 6: Verify cost estimates use current Claude pricing (input vs output tokens)
features.notes: Need to investigate: where is token/cost data? Options: (a) transcript JSONL may have usage fields, (b) ~/.claude/projects/ session metadata, (c) hook payloads. Research needed before design.

features.description: Mobile-accessible dashboard. Responsive CSS for phone/tablet use. Expose server beyond localhost via Tailscale so it's reachable from mobile devices on the same tailnet.
verification_steps: Step 1: Open dashboard on a phone (via Tailscale IP)
Step 2: Verify session cards stack vertically and are readable
Step 3: Verify permission approve/deny buttons are tap-friendly (min 44px touch target)
Step 4: Verify transcript panel is usable on mobile
Step 5: Verify sidebar collapses to hamburger menu
Step 6: Test on both portrait and landscape orientations
features.notes: Server currently binds to localhost only. Need to add config option to bind to 0.0.0.0 or Tailscale IP. Security consideration: Tailscale is trusted, but should still be opt-in.

features.description: Drag-and-drop files to sessions. Drag files onto a session card or use a file picker to inject file content into a dashboard-managed session's context. File content sent as the next message or appended to prompt.
verification_steps: Step 1: Start a dashboard-managed session
Step 2: Drag a .ts file onto the session card (or transcript panel)
Step 3: Verify the file name appears in the input bar as an attachment indicator
Step 4: Type 'Review this file' and send
Step 5: Verify Claude receives the file content and responds appropriately
Step 6: Test with multiple file types (.ts, .json, .md, .png)
features.notes: Only viable for dashboard-managed sessions. For terminal sessions, show 'drag to terminal' guidance instead. Need to decide: include file content inline in prompt, or reference by path?
